_schema._comment: HOW TO ADD ITEMS — read this before editing

_fields.id: Unique string key. Use kebab-case (e.g. 'iron-ore'). Must match exactly when referenced in recipes.
_fields.name: Display name shown in the UI (e.g. 'Iron Ore').
_fields.category: Optional grouping label shown in search (e.g. 'Metal', 'Weapon', 'Potion'). Use empty string '' to omit.
_fields.craftable: true = this item can be crafted (has a recipe). false = raw material, no recipe.
_fields.recipe: Array of ingredients needed to craft ONE unit of this item. Only include if craftable=true. Omit or set to [] if craftable=false.
_fields.recipe[].itemId: The 'id' of the ingredient item. Must exist in this file.
_fields.recipe[].qty: How many units of this ingredient are needed per craft of the parent item.

_tips: To add a raw material: set craftable=false and omit recipe.
To add a craftable item: set craftable=true and fill recipe[].
IDs are case-sensitive. 'Iron-Ore' and 'iron-ore' are different.
You can nest arbitrarily deep — the app handles multi-level breakdown automatically.
Avoid circular references (A needs B needs A). The app does not guard against infinite loops.
